summary_one_line: Input containing a ZWJ (U+200D) glyph that is last-in-buffer or followed by a non-Extended_Pictographic codepoint, taking the false branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A buffer whose decoded codepoint sequence contains a ZWJ (joiner) glyph that is either at the final position (i+1 >= count) or whose following codepoint is NOT an Emoji Extended_Pictographic, so the conjunct at line 491 short-circuits to false.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive resolves the false branch in all 10/10 trials; its winning seeds are dominated by long runs of repeated filler bytes (e.g. 0x85, 0x32, 0x95) and trailing 0d 20 sequences that decode into multi-glyph buffers where a ZWJ appears as the trailing/penultimate glyph, so i+1 reaches count or the next codepoint is not Extended_Pictographic.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks the false branch in 9/10 trials (resolved only 1/10); the I2S substitution mutator steers its inputs toward CMP-matched operands so it tends to satisfy the true-branch condition (ZWJ followed by an Extended_Pictographic), keeping i+1<count AND the pictographic test true, which is exactly the blocked-side L behavior shown at line 491 (L true=11, false=0).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The winner is naive, which has NO comparison introspection — it explores the false branch purely by havoc byte mutation (havoc-only) that happens to produce ZWJ-terminal or ZWJ-then-non-pictographic buffers. The loser cmplog's input-to-state substitution actively biases bytes toward the operands recorded at the line-492 pictographic comparison, pulling it onto the true side and starving the false branch; thus the divergence is attributable to the ABSENCE of comparison steering in naive, i.e. naive's lucky havoc exploration is what enables the winning side.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is naive>cmplog with delta I2S; naive is the winner and cmplog the loser.
naive resolves the false branch in 10/10 trials while cmplog blocks 9/10.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
winner: resolved=10/10  blocked=0  unreached=0
  loser:  resolved=1/10  blocked=9  unreached=0

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the conjunct guarding the next-codepoint pictographic test; the false branch is the globally blocked side.
The blocker line is a conjunction that first checks i+1<count then the next codepoint's Extended_Pictographic status, reached only inside the ZWJ branch.
The ZWJ branch gates the blocker; only ZWJ glyphs reach line 491.
Loser-blocking inputs take the true branch at line 491 (L true=11, false=0), i.e. ZWJ followed in-range by an Extended_Pictographic codepoint.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 491  T=35 F=10  T=11 F=0  if (i + 1 < count &&  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds contain long runs of repeated filler bytes and trailing 0d 20 sequences.
naive has no I2S; it mutates purely with the havoc + token stack.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutes a recorded CMP operand into the input at matching offsets, steering bytes toward the compared value.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is havoc-only (the WINNER is naive, which has no I2S/CMP introspection per MECHANISM CONTEXT), so the I2SRandReplace lineage query is not applicable. The divergence is attributable to the absence of I2S steering in naive rather than a substitution mutator; no dash-row floor signal is expected or required on the winning naive seeds (naive produces zero dash rows by construction).

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the next-codepoint Extended_Pictographic CMP at line 492 is the ONLY gate and cmplog still resolves the false branch as fast as naive — showing I2S steering does not bias cmplog onto the true side and that naive's advantage is not from lacking that steering.

weakest_evidence_point: The claim that cmplog's I2S steering specifically pulls inputs onto the true (pictographic) side is inferred from the L-side branch counts and CMP mechanism, not directly observed in seed lineage.
